summary: Los nuevos ataques cruzados entre EEUU e irán enturbian otra vez los ánimos de los inversores. Los mensajes de Donald Trump enfrían además el optimismo sobre una próxima reapertura de Ormuz, y el petróleo gira al alza. El Ibex apunta a la baja y se distancia de sus récords. Leer

title: Oportunidades entre los pequeños valores de la Bolsa
summary: Oryzon, Atrys, Dia, Dominion, Cirsa y Amper cuentan con un amplio respaldo de analistas y tienen un potencial de entre el 20% y el 140%. Leer

title: Por qué se agrava la caída del bitcoin
summary: La mayor de las criptomonedas, aplicando términos bursátiles, se adentra ya técnicamente en fase de corrección. El bache, esta vez, se ve agravado por el drástico cambio de rumbo en los flujos procedentes de los ETF. Leer

title: US ambassador backs Trump's criticism of Spain's government
summary: Drawing a distinction between the Spanish people and government, the US's new Ambassador to Spain has backed Trump's recent criticisms of the Sánchez administration just days after meeting the country's far-right leader.

title: Today in Spain: A roundup of the latest news on Thursday
summary: Sánchez makes Vatican visit, March mortgage approvals highest in 16 years, Vox councillor in Valencia faces three years in prison, and more news from Spain on Thursday 28th May.

title: Europe heatwave a 'brutal reminder' of climate change
summary: The UN climate chief said Wednesday that a record-breaking early heatwave scorching a swathe of western Europe was "a brutal reminder of the spiralling impacts of the climate crisis".

title: Spain's PM keeps backing ex-leader targeted in corruption probe
summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said Wednesday there was "no reason" to withdraw support for his predecessor José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero, who is under investigation for corruption, following the publication of new details this week about the case.

title: Police enter Spain's ruling party HQ as corruption probe deepens
summary: Spanish police entered the Madrid headquarters of the ruling Socialist Party on Wednesday to demand documents as part of a corruption investigation into a former party member, judicial sources said.
